text:
To mark the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project we are releasing a new high-performance Artificial Intelligence / Theorem Proving system for formal mathematics, capable of bringing the field of formal mathematics to a new era. Experiments show that our system is able to fully automatically prove some 60% of the theorems in the Mizar Mathematical Library (MML) in the “hammer” mode, where all it gets is the statement of a theorem. Increasing this percentage to 75% is possible when the additional premises that a human proofwriter needs to introduce from the knowledge base in order to prove a theorem are provided.

We present in detail the methods and the large-scale experiments that led to the aforementioned results. Some key technical contributions include: 1) the combination of the E prover with some machine learning improvements that we implemented (as part of the ENIGMA and Deepire projects) 2) the premise selection learning-based techniques 3) an incremental “prove-learn” loop for training increasingly complex neural and gradient-boosted models by generating millions of automated proofs. We finish the paper with some illustrations of fully automatically solvable Mizar problems that have been shown to be hard over the last decade.
